title: thelypodium lasiophyllum var. utahense
intro: Thelypodium lasiophyllum var. utahense is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Thelypodium lasiophyllum plant, a member of the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Thelypodium lasiophyllum var. utahense include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
